title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Program Brief: Byte Size Budget
summary: The command concatenates strings into paths, follows any symlink, accepts directories and devices, reads before checking size, and reports every failure as file not found. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the byte size budget increment in bounded file-admission policy. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Reject inputs beyond a declared byte budget before unbounded allocation and preserve actual versus allowed size evidence.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_paths_file_admission_4(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

Competency probe: Reject inputs beyond a declared byte budget before unbounded allocation and preserve actual versus allowed size evidence.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Byte Size Budget model · Theory Byte Size Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Character count is an adequate pre-read file-size limit.
